items.ja: 特別粥と12種類の精進料理（平日）
items.en: Special rice porridge with 12 kinds of shojin (Buddhist vegetarian) dishes — weekdays
items.note: Shojin ryori is fully plant-based (no meat/fish/animal products; dashi from kombu/shiitake), so it is vegan by tradition. NOT automatically gluten-free: the rice porridge (okayu) itself is GF, but among the 12 side dishes several will be seasoned with wheat-containing soy sauce (醤油) or wheat-based seitan/fu (麩). Celiac must ask which of the 12 dishes contain 醤油/小麦/麩 and request they be omitted or served plain; okayu porridge is the safe base.

items.en: Cold buckwheat noodles with dipping sauce
items.note: House standard is 二八 (nihachi) soba = ~80% buckwheat, 20% WHEAT flour — contains gluten. Dipping tsuyu also contains wheat soy sauce and bonito dashi. Avoid; ask instead about the 十割 (juwari) 100% buckwheat soba below.

items.en: Cold buckwheat noodles with nori, dipping sauce
items.note: Nihachi (wheat-blended) noodles — contains gluten. Tsuyu contains wheat soy sauce and bonito dashi.

The noodles are 十割 = 100% buckwheat, no wheat flour — the only potentially safe noodle here, but limited to ~10 servings a day (arrive early). NOT certified GF: the same kitchen and boiling water handle wheat-blend nihachi soba (cross-contamination), and the dipping tsuyu contains wheat soy sauce + dashi. Celiac should confirm the noodles are truly wheat-free that day, ask for a freshly boiled batch, and skip the tsuyu or bring own tamari + grated daikon/wasabi. Not vegan unless dashi/tsuyu avoided (bonito).
Nihachi wheat-blend noodles in a wheat-soy-sauce + bonito broth — contains gluten. Avoid.
Double wheat: wheat-blend noodles AND wheat-flour tempura batter, plus wheat-soy broth. Avoid.

items.ja: 善光寺ケンピ 信州味噌 焙煎一味
items.romaji: zenkoji kenpi shinshu miso baisen ichimi
items.note: Fried sweet-potato sticks are GF on their own, but the Shinshu-miso seasoning may contain barley or wheat — ask, or choose a plain/salt kenpi instead.

items.ja: 焼き芋ブリュレ アイスのせ
items.en: Baked sweet potato brulee topped with ice cream
items.note: Sweet potato base is GF but ice cream and any biscuit/cookie topping may contain wheat — ask. Dairy.

items.ja: 蜜芋ふたいろモンブラン
items.note: Mont Blanc — ask about the base (tart/cookie base contains wheat). Price ¥800–860. Dairy.

items.en: Kaiseki multi-course in a lacquer box, 'Shun'
items.note: Seasonal multi-course kaiseki (also available as takeout). As a traditional ryotei they can accommodate celiac needs if arranged when reserving: ask for grilled fish with salt (塩, not tare/soy glaze), sashimi with your own tamari, plain steamed rice, and vegetables seasoned with salt rather than soy. Note: swapping in 信州そば soba (+¥330) is wheat-blend — avoid.

items.en: Kaiseki multi-course in a lacquer box, 'Suehiro'
items.note: Larger seasonal kaiseki course. Same approach: arrange in advance and request grilled fish with salt (not tare), sashimi with your own tamari, plain rice, and salt-seasoned vegetables.

items.en: Lunch kaiseki 'Yuga' (6-course omakase: sobagaki, karasumi soba, hassun, warm dish, ten-juu soba, matcha & dried sweets)
items.note: Omakase soba kaiseki. Soba is almost certainly a buckwheat-wheat blend, NOT gluten-free — ask whether a 十割 juwari (100% buckwheat) noodle can be substituted. Grilled/hassun items can be requested with salt (塩) not soy-based tare, and any sashimi with your own tamari. Course is fixed; discuss all restrictions when reserving.

items.en: Dinner kaiseki 'Manpuku' (appetizer, karasumi soba, hassun, warm dish, ten-juu soba, dessert, matcha)
items.note: Same cautions as lunch: soba noodle is wheat-blend unless juwari confirmed; ask for grilled items with salt and bring tamari for any raw fish. Reserve with restrictions stated in advance.

items.en: Dinner kaiseki 'Tsumugi' (9-course: appetizer, karasumi soba, hassun, soba sushi, meat dish, ten-juu soba, dessert, matcha)
items.note: Longest course; includes soba sushi and soba noodle (wheat-blend unless juwari). Ask what can be swapped/plated GF-safe (grilled fish with salt, plain components). Not a reliable GF meal without advance arrangement.

items.en: Dinner kaiseki 'Shifuku' (premium 8-course with house-milled ten-juu soba)
items.note: Premium omakase. Same soba caution. Additional soba serving ¥1,100. Discuss celiac needs at reservation.

items.en: Pork loin cutlet set meal (with rice, miso soup, shredded cabbage)
items.note: The katsu is panko-breaded (WHEAT) and deep-fried in a shared fryer — not GF. However the shop is unusual: the chef brings out the whole SPF-pork block and you choose your cut, so you can ASK whether they will grill/pan-sear your chosen pork plain with salt (塩) instead of breading. Within a set, plain rice (ご飯) and shredded cabbage (キャベツ) are GF; skip the tonkatsu sauce (wheat). Confirm sauce/dressing before eating.

items.ja: 厚切りロースかつ／千代幻豚ロースかつ
items.en: Thick-cut (approx. 5cm) or Chiyogenton-brand pork loin cutlet set
items.note: Premium thick cut, still panko-breaded (wheat) and fried in shared oil. Same ask applies — request the raw cut prepared unbreaded with salt if the kitchen will accommodate. Not a default GF option.

items.en: Traditional Japanese shojin (Buddhist vegetarian) lunch set
items.note: Shojin ryori is plant-based (no meat, fish, or the five pungent roots) — effectively vegan. NOT automatically GF: dishes commonly use soy sauce (wheat) and may include 麩 fu (wheat gluten) or tempura. Ask for tamari instead of soy sauce and for fu/battered items to be omitted. Reserve ~1 week ahead and state celiac needs. Naturally GF-friendly items to ask for: goma-dofu (sesame tofu), simmered vegetables, plain rice.

Modern Italian-style shojin. Plant-based in principle but confirm no dairy/cheese (mark vegan only after asking). Italian format likely involves pasta/bread (wheat) — ask what can be plated GF and request tamari over soy sauce. Reservation required.

Plant-based Buddhist cuisine using seasonal, partly pesticide-free vegetables grown by the head priest — effectively vegan. NOT automatically GF: soy sauce (wheat) and wheat gluten (fu) are common. Ask for tamari and omission of fu/tempura. House-made goma-dofu (sesame tofu, typically sesame + kuzu starch) is a naturally GF-and-vegan item to request. Reserve 3 days ahead with restrictions stated.

Multi-course seasonal shojin kaiseki (appetizers, house-made sesame tofu, simmered vegetables). Same cautions: request tamari not soy sauce, avoid fu/battered items. Discuss celiac needs at reservation; content varies by season.

items.ja: 黒カレー（信州牛ゴロゴロ）
items.en: Black curry with chunky Shinshu beef
items.note: Spice-forward curry (not standard boxed roux). Japanese curries usually contain wheat flour as thickener — ASK whether this one is flour-free/GF. Contains beef.

items.ja: 赤カレー（バターチキン）
items.note: Butter chicken is often flourless but contains dairy. Ask whether any wheat flour is used. Not vegan (chicken + butter/cream).

items.ja: 緑カレー（ガラムマサラ・シュリンプ）
items.note: Coconut/spice-based curries are frequently GF — ask to confirm no wheat thickener. Contains shrimp.

items.ja: 北海道産オーブンポテト 万願寺サムライソース添え
items.romaji: Hokkaido-san oven potato, Manganji samurai sauce
items.en: Oven-roasted Hokkaido potatoes with Manganji-pepper 'samurai' sauce
items.note: Potatoes are naturally GF — ask about the sauce (samurai/harissa sauces can contain wheat or dairy). Confirm both GF and vegan status of the sauce.

items.ja: トルティーヤチップス 万願寺ハリッサソース添え
items.en: Tortilla chips with Manganji-pepper harissa sauce
items.note: Corn tortilla chips are often GF but some blends add wheat — ask. Harissa is usually vegan; confirm.
Macarons are made with almond flour (no wheat) so often naturally GF — ask about cross-contamination in the kitchen. Contains egg white (not vegan).

items.ja: スパイス・ジェラート
items.note: Gelato base is GF-friendly but confirm no cookie/wheat mix-ins; ask which flavors. Dairy-based (not vegan).

items.ja: クラシカル・パンケーキ
items.note: Wheat-flour pancakes — not GF.
Amazake is made from fermented rice (usually GF) with soy milk — vegan. Ask whether it is rice-koji amazake (GF) versus a sake-lees (sakekasu) version, which can contain trace wheat.

items.en: Pasta lunch course (appetizer, soup, pasta, house bread, drink)
items.note: Reservation-only omakase Italian built on Shinshu seasonal vegetables — a good place to negotiate. The pasta and house bread are wheat, but ask whether the chef can plate the vegetable-forward antipasti/soup GF and swap pasta for a risotto or grilled-vegetable dish. Confirm no wheat in dressings/soup. Veg-forward so vegan may be possible on request.
Longer seasonal omakase lunch. Same negotiation: ask chef in advance to build a GF (and/or vegan) menu around Shinshu vegetables, avoiding pasta/bread and wheat-thickened sauces.

Reservation-only seasonal dinner omakase. Because the kitchen already cooks to order around vegetables, ask when booking whether they can prepare a fully GF course (grilled fish/vegetables with salt or olive oil, no pasta/bread/wheat sauce). State celiac clearly.

items.ja: 会席料理 菊コース
items.note: Multi-course kaiseki; menu changes monthly so components vary. Celiac-negotiable within the course: ask for sashimi served with your own tamari (no house soy sauce), grilled fish finished with salt 塩 not tare/teriyaki, and plain steamed rice. Caution: kaiseki dashi, ponzu and simmered dishes typically contain soy sauce (wheat) — confirm each dish. Vegetable/tofu components can often be prepared without soy on request.

items.ja: 会席料理 桔梗コース
items.note: Larger kaiseki course. Same negotiation as Kiku: sashimi with own tamari, grilled fish with salt not tare, plain rice. Confirm dashi/simmered items — usually contain soy sauce (wheat).

items.ja: 会席料理 葵コース
items.note: Kaiseki course. Negotiable components: sashimi with own tamari, salt-grilled fish (塩, no tare), plain rice, plain-cooked seasonal vegetables. Verify each course element for soy sauce.

items.ja: 会席料理 茜コース
items.note: Higher-tier kaiseki course. Ask for sashimi with own tamari, grilled fish with salt not sauce, plain rice. Dashi/simmered/dressed dishes commonly use soy sauce (wheat) — confirm.

items.ja: 会席料理 灯コース
items.note: Premium kaiseki course. Same celiac negotiation applies; confirm dashi and any dressed/simmered dishes for soy sauce (wheat).

items.ja: 会席料理 禅コース
items.note: Top kaiseki course. Ask kitchen to build safe components: sashimi with own tamari, salt-grilled fish, plain rice, plainly prepared vegetables. Verify each dish — kaiseki relies heavily on soy-based seasonings (wheat).
